mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <45% of co-occurring growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months where industrial production rises, regardless of the macro regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, persistent inflationary pressure forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 230.0 over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate revenue fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 shows positive growth, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain stress, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where industrial production drops by more than 0.5 percent.
